name: Chakra UI
description: Accessible React components for building high-quality web apps and design systems

initial-release.comment: Created by Segun Adebayo in 2019. Repository created August 17, 2019.

styling-approach.comment: Built with Emotion (CSS-in-JS). v3 uses Panda CSS under the hood. Supports CSS variables and design tokens.

component-model.comment: Primarily styled components with ready-to-use visuals. Supports unstyled mode for some components. Can be used in headless fashion via style overrides.

typescript.comment: Written in TypeScript (82.0% of codebase). First-class type definitions included.

ssr-compatible.comment: Works with Next.js, Remix, Gatsby, and other SSR frameworks

rsc-compatible.comment: Official support for Next.js React Server Components introduced in v3

design-system.comment: Custom design system with its own design philosophy. Not based on Material Design, Ant Design, or other existing systems.

dark-mode.comment: Built-in dark mode support using semantic tokens and ColorModeProvider. Relies on next-themes library for React apps.

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming with customizable tokens (colors, spacing, typography, etc.) and semantic tokens for contextual theming

css-variables.comment: Uses CSS custom properties for theming. Token values are converted to CSS variables with configurable prefix.

design-tokens.comment: Design token system influenced by W3C Token Format. Supports semantic tokens that change based on conditions like color mode.

rtl-support.comment: Built-in RTL support for Arabic, Hebrew, and other right-to-left languages. Supports both stylis-plugin-rtl and RTL-aware style props.

component-count.comment: Approximately 60+ distinct components across layout, forms, data display, feedback, overlays, navigation, and typography categories. v3 added 25+ new components including ColorPicker, DatePicker, TreeView, Carousel, Rating, TagsInput, and more.

text-input.comment: Input component with variants for text, password, email, search, etc.

textarea.comment: Multi-line text input with auto-resize capability

select.comment: Native select component for single selection

multi-select.comment: Multi-select available through Combobox component with multiple selection mode

combobox.comment: Searchable/filterable select with autocomplete. Added in v3 with recipe refactoring for better padding management.

checkbox.comment: Single checkbox and checkbox groups. Also includes Checkbox Card variant added in v3.

switch-toggle.comment: Switch component for boolean toggle values

slider.comment: Range slider with single and dual-thumb support

number-input.comment: Numeric input with increment/decrement stepper buttons

tag-input.comment: TagsInput component for multiple tags/tokens with add/remove and keyboard navigation. Added in v3.30.

pin-input.comment: OTP/PIN code input with individual digit boxes

color-picker.comment: Color Picker component added in v3. Full color selection UI with picker interface and Color Swatch component.

date-picker.comment: DatePicker component from Ark UI added in v3. Previously required third-party libraries like chakra-dayzed-datepicker.

time-picker.comment: No dedicated TimePicker component. Third-party packages like chakra-datetime-picker offer datetime functionality.

date-range-picker.comment: DatePicker component exists but dedicated range picker not explicitly documented. May be possible with DatePicker configuration.

file-upload.comment: File Upload component with preview support added in v3

dropzone.comment: File Upload component exists but dedicated drag-and-drop Dropzone not explicitly documented as separate component

rating-input.comment: Rating component for star ratings and reviews. Visual rating input added in v3.

input-groups.comment: InputGroup, InputLeftAddon, InputRightAddon, InputLeftElement, InputRightElement for combining inputs with icons, buttons, and text

input-with-dropdown.comment: Possible via InputGroup composition or Combobox component

split-button.comment: No dedicated split button component. Can be built using ButtonGroup and Menu composition.

table.comment: Basic data table with sticky header support, interactive hover, striped variants

data-table.comment: Basic Table component exists but no advanced DataTable with built-in sorting/filtering/pagination. Users typically integrate with TanStack Table for advanced features.

virtual-infinite-list.comment: No built-in virtualized scrolling. Users integrate with react-window or react-virtualized for large lists.

tree-view.comment: TreeView component with expandable nodes, lazy loading, disabled states, and programmatic control. Added in v3.

list-view.comment: List component with ordered and unordered variants, icons, and custom styling

card.comment: Card component with Header, Body, Footer sub-components

avatar.comment: Avatar component with fallback, group, and badge support

tag-chip-pill.comment: Tag component (also called chip/pill) with closable and clickable variants

timeline.comment: Timeline component for vertical timeline of events. Added in v3.

calendar.comment: DatePicker includes calendar view for selection but no standalone Calendar component for month/week display views documented

alert.comment: Static alert/banner component with multiple variants and status types

toast-notification.comment: Toast component for temporary popup notifications with positioning and stacking

progress-bar.comment: Linear progress bar for determinate progress indication

spinner-loader.comment: Spinner component for indeterminate loading states

skeleton.comment: Skeleton placeholder component for loading content

empty-state.comment: Empty State component for empty content areas. Added in v3.

modal-dialog.comment: Modal/Dialog component with focus trapping and proper ARIA attributes

drawer-sheet.comment: Drawer component that slides in from edge (top, right, bottom, left)

popover.comment: Popover component for floating content anchored to trigger

tooltip.comment: Tooltip component for hover hint text

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu component with MenuButton, MenuList, MenuItem for action menus

context-menu.comment: Menu.ContextTrigger component for right-click context menus. Official implementation guide available.

command-palette.comment: No built-in command palette (⌘K style) component. Can be built using Modal, Input, and Menu composition.

tabs.comment: Tabs component with horizontal tab navigation and content switching

vertical-tabs.comment: Tabs component supports vertical orientation via orientation prop

pagination.comment: Pagination component for page navigation controls. Added in v3.

stepper.comment: Steps/Stepper component for multi-step process indication with useSteps hook

navigation-menu.comment: Menu component supports nested menus for site navigation with dropdowns

sidebar.comment: No dedicated Sidebar component. Built using composition of Drawer (mobile) and Box/Flex (desktop) components. Common pattern documented in community guides.

bottom-navigation.comment: No dedicated bottom navigation component. Can be built using Flex/Box with positioning.

grid.comment: Grid and SimpleGrid components for CSS grid layouts

stack.comment: Stack, HStack, VStack for flex-based vertical/horizontal stacking with spacing

container.comment: Container component for centered, max-width content

aspect-ratio.comment: AspectRatio component to maintain aspect ratio containers

collapsible.comment: Collapsible component for expandable/collapsible content. Also Accordion for grouped collapsibles.

resizable.comment: No dedicated resizable component. Community solutions exist for resizable panels.

splitter.comment: Splitter component for resizable split panes. Supports horizontal, vertical, multi-panel, controlled, and store-driven configurations. Announced November 22, 2025.

rich-text-editors.comment: No official rich text editor integrations. Components work with any React-based editor but no documented official bindings.

code-editors.comment: CodeBlock component added in v3.26 with Prism and Shiki integration for syntax highlighting

chart-libraries.comment: @chakra-ui/charts package provides Chart components. Works well with Recharts. No official documentation for other chart libraries.

form-libraries.comment: Well-documented integration with React Hook Form. Community packages like react-hook-form-chakra-fields and chakra-ui-react-hook-form provide bindings. Works with Yup and Zod for validation.

animation-libraries.comment: Uses Framer Motion internally for component animations. Official documentation for custom Framer Motion integration using chakra factory.

icon-libraries.comment: Removed @chakra-ui/icons in v3. Now recommends lucide-react (preferred) or react-icons. Icon component works with any React icon library.

date-libraries.comment: DatePicker component in v3 likely uses date-fns internally. Third-party packages support date-fns, dayjs, and Dayzed.

aria-compliant.comment: All components follow WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper aria-* attributes. Each component has accessibility.md report.

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard navigation support across all interactive components including arrow key navigation in menus/tabs

screen-reader-tested.comment: Components built with semantic HTML and tested with screen readers. Testimonials confirm screen reader compatibility.

focus-management.comment: Proper focus trapping in modals, focus restoration, and visual focus indicators throughout

wcag-level.comment: Adheres to WCAG 2.0/2.1 standards. Theme system enforces 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text (WCAG AA compliance).

accessibility-rating.comment: Excellent accessibility implementation. Built accessibility-first from ground up with WAI-ARIA compliance, screen reader testing, semantic HTML, focus management, and documented a11y reports per component.

bundle-size-full.comment: 246 KB minified + gzipped for @chakra-ui/react@3.26.0. Includes 8 dependencies. Framer Motion adds ~31 KB.

bundle-size-core.comment: Tree-shakeable but no distinct 'core' bundle. Importing individual components can reduce bundle by 20-40KB in typical applications.

tree-shakeable.comment: Supports tree-shaking with named imports. Modern bundlers eliminate unused exports through dead code elimination.

zero-runtime.comment: Uses CSS-in-JS with Emotion runtime. v3 uses Panda CSS which is more optimized but not zero-runtime.

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be lazy-loaded on demand. TreeView specifically supports lazy loading for children nodes.

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation with comprehensive component API reference, live interactive examples, guides, recipes, migration paths, accessibility reports, and integration examples.

interactive-docs.comment: Live component playground/sandbox embedded in documentation with editable code examples

storybook.comment: No official public Storybook. Interactive examples embedded directly in documentation instead.

figma-kit.comment: Official Chakra UI v3 Figma Kit available free on Figma Community. Includes components, variants, sizes, and design tokens. FigPilot plugin converts Figma designs to React code.

cli-generator.comment: Official @chakra-ui/cli for typegen (generate theme typings), snippet (add component snippets), blocks (add Pro blocks), and eject (customize theme). Community tool create-chakra scaffolds projects.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Approximately 1,000,043 weekly downloads for @chakra-ui/react@3.30.0 as of December 2025. Website reports 4.3M monthly downloads.

active-maintenance.comment: Very active maintenance with regular releases. Latest release November 22, 2025. Active development on v3 with continuous improvements.

commercial-support.comment: Chakra UI Pro available at $19/month with premium components, blocks, page templates, and priority support. Used by companies like Tinder, Spotify, and Framer.

style-override.comment: Multiple override methods: style props (inline), sx prop, className, styled API via chakra factory, and CSS variables for theming

compound-components.comment: Extensive use of compound component pattern (e.g., Menu.Button, Menu.List, Menu.Item, Card.Header, Card.Body, Table.Root, Table.Header)

render-props.comment: Several components support render props for customization (e.g., Select, Combobox render functions)

slots.comment: Components support slot-based composition pattern for content injection (similar to named slots)

unstyled-mode.comment: Many components offer 'unstyled' variant. Can override styles completely via style props and CSS variables.
